details: Launched in 2018, the scheme "Incentive Scheme in Civil Service" by the Social Welfare Department, Government of Chhattisgarh aims to encourage bright and meritorious persons with disabilities to pass competitive exams in the field of civil services. Through this scheme, financial support is provided to help with upcoming preparations. The scheme benefits include lump sum amounts on passing the different stages of the Union/Chhattisgarh Public Service Commission Competitive Examinations. The scheme is implemented by the Social Welfare Department.
benefits: ₹20,000/- lump sum on passing the Preliminary Examination.
₹30,000/- lump sum on passing the Mains Examination.
₹50,000/- lump sum on selection in Union/Chhattisgarh Public Service Commission.
eligibility: The applicant should be a resident of Chhattisgarh.
The applicant should have a disability of 40% or above.
The applicant should have passed the Union or Chhattisgarh Public Service Commission Competitive Examination.

offline: The interested applicant should take print of the

In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, paste the passport-sized photograph (signed across, if required), and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required).

Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with the documents, within the prescribed period (if any), to the concerned staff at the District Office of the Joint/Deputy Director, Social Welfare Department.

documents_required: Proof of Passing in Union/Chhattisgarh Public Service Commission Competitive Examinations

faqs.question: What is the minimum percentage of disability required to be eligible for the financial support scheme?
faqs.answer: Applicants must have a disability of 40% or more to qualify for the financial support provided under this scheme.

faqs.question: Is there a residency requirement to apply for this financial support scheme?
faqs.answer: Yes, only residents of Chhattisgarh are eligible to apply for this financial assistance scheme.

faqs.question: What documents are required to prove my residency for the scheme application?
faqs.answer: You need to submit a valid residence proof document as part of your application to confirm your eligibility.

faqs.question: What financial benefit is provided for passing the preliminary examination under this scheme?
faqs.answer: A lump sum amount of ₹20,000 is awarded to applicants who pass the preliminary examination.

faqs.question: What is the financial incentive for passing the mains examination under this scheme?
faqs.answer: Applicants who pass the mains examination receive a lump sum amount of ₹30,000 as financial support.

faqs.question: What financial benefit is provided if I am selected in the Union/Chhattisgarh Public Service Commission Competitive Examinations?
faqs.answer: If selected, you will receive a lump sum amount of ₹50,000 as part of the financial assistance scheme.

faqs.question: Do I need to provide proof of passing the competitive examinations to apply for this scheme?
faqs.answer: Yes, you must submit proof of passing the Union/Chhattisgarh Public Service Commission Competitive Examinations to be eligible.

faqs.question: Is a photograph required to be submitted with the application form?
faqs.answer: Yes, a passport-sized photograph must be pasted on the application form, signed across if required.

faqs.question: Where should I submit the completed application form and documents?
faqs.answer: The duly filled application form and documents must be submitted to the District Office of the Joint/Deputy Director, Social Welfare Department.

faqs.question: What is the purpose of providing financial support under this scheme?
faqs.answer: The scheme aims to encourage persons with disabilities to prepare for and pass competitive exams in civil services.

faqs.question: What type of competitive exams are covered under this financial support scheme?
faqs.answer: The scheme covers competitive exams conducted by the Union and Chhattisgarh Public Service Commissions.

faqs.question: Is there any age limit mentioned for applying to this financial support scheme?
faqs.answer: No specific age limit is mentioned in the provided information for applying to this scheme.
